selected_topic: First dinosaur fossil discovered in Antarctica
news_angle: Scientists have identified the first-ever dinosaur fossil found on Antarctica, revealing it belonged to a titanosaur, a giant herbivorous dinosaur.
why_this_is_news: This discovery is the first of its kind on Antarctica, expanding our understanding of dinosaur distribution and the prehistoric environment of the continent.
specific_action: discovered and identified as a titanosaur
scene_metaphor: Hunk Mao as an adventurer cat in icy explorer gear, striking a dynamic pose on a snowy Antarctic landscape with a titanosaur skeleton looming impressively behind him, looking both cool and confident as he 'unlocks' a lost prehistoric secret.
image_prompt: A stylish, athletic orange tabby cat with fluffy cheek fur and sharp tabby markings wearing a black hoodie, goggles, and a warm explorer hat. He stands confidently on a snowy Antarctic backdrop with a gigantic titanosaur skeleton fossil behind him, illuminated by cold blue light. The cat holds a fossil bone in one paw and sports a mischievous grin amid dynamic, cinematic, anime-inspired style with strong energetic pose and oversized expressive eyes.
easter_eggs: small paw print fossil imprint on the snow near cat
tiny sci-fi drone hovering above scouting the area
caption: Scientists discovered the first dinosaur fossil ever found in Antarctica—a massive titanosaur. Looks like even the coldest places can't hide giant secrets from this cool cat explorer!
risk_notes: Ensure Antarctic scenery avoids any real logos or wildlife; keep dinosaur skeletal design scientifically inspired but not overly technical to maintain broad appeal.
